summary: A tiny gas-rich emirate that punches above its weight through Al Jazeera, massive sovereign wealth, hosting of the US airbase at Al-Udeid, and diplomacy that talks to everyone — including Hamas, the Taliban, and Iran — simultaneously.

history.event: Current Emir (Hamad) takes power in coup
history.detail: Son overthrows father; rapid modernisation begins.
Transforms Arab media; broadcasts voices previously suppressed.
Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Egypt blockade Qatar for 3.5 years over Al Jazeera and Iran ties.
First World Cup in Middle East; $200B+ spending; migrant worker deaths controversy.

key_doctrines: Soft power via Al Jazeera and sports

leverage_tools: QIA investments globally (Heathrow, Harrods, PSG, Barclays)

resources.oil_gas: World's 3rd largest natural gas reserves (North Dome — shared with Iran); major LNG exporter

follow_the_money.detail: $475B sovereign wealth in Heathrow, Harrods, PSG football, Barclays, Volkswagen
Qatar pays Hamas $30M/month through UN-approved mechanism; also funds Gaza reconstruction
Europe scrambled for Qatari LNG after Russia cut gas 2022; huge leverage

theories_disputed.detail: FBI/Swiss investigations confirmed bribes paid to FIFA officials; Qatar denies direct state involvement.

connections.detail: Al-Udeid Air Base — largest US base in Middle East (10,000 troops)
Qatar funds Hamas and Gaza reconstruction; key mediation role
Share North Dome gas field; maintained relations during blockade
Turkish troops deployed in Qatar during Saudi blockade
